data.title: Xinhua News Agency: Tensions in the Middle East have spilled over into the Asia-Pacific region, raising economic concerns and prompting calls for diplomatic mediation.
data.description: The ongoing military conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran continues to impact the Asia-Pacific region, triggering economic uncertainty and energy security alarms. Many governments and regional organizations have called for an immediate cessation of military operations. Julia Roknifard, a senior lecturer at Taylor's University in Malaysia, points out that Southeast Asia may face negative impacts such as trade disruptions and rising energy costs. A survey by the Malaysian Foreign Trade Development Council shows that nearly 64% of Malaysian businesses expect to be affected by the conflict, mainly involving transportation delays, increased shipping and insurance costs, and rising raw material prices linked to crude oil. Retail oil prices in Cambodia rose over the past week, with key garment and agricultural sectors facing higher production and logistics costs. Sri Lankan President Anura Kumara Dissanayake warned that the conflict could impact the country's economy. South Korea's KOSPI and KOSDAQ indices fell 12.06% and 14% respectively on Wednesday before rebounding on Thursday. International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva stated in Bangkok on Thursday that if the conflict continues, it could affect global energy prices, market sentiment, economic growth, and inflation. Countries are actively responding to the impact. Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet emphasized that ASEAN must strengthen regional resilience through energy diversification and deeper economic integration. Vietnam established a special task force to monitor the energy market, the Philippines stated it has 50 to 60 days' worth of oil reserves, and South Korea issued a "watch" alert for crude oil and natural gas. Australia and New Zealand began assisting their citizens in evacuating from the Middle East. On the diplomatic front, ASEAN foreign ministers issued a joint statement on March 4 calling for an immediate ceasefire. Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi spoke with the foreign ministers of Russia, Iran, Israel, Saudi Arabia, and other countries, emphasizing adherence to the UN Charter and opposition to the arbitrary use of force. China will also send its special envoy for Middle East affairs, Zhai Jun, to the region to promote de-escalation.

data.title: Binance, CZ Cleared in US Civil Suit Over Alleged Terror Financing
data.description:
A US federal judge dismissed a lawsuit accusing Binance and Changpeng Zhao of enabling crypto transactions tied to terrorist attacks.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/binance-cz-cleared-in-us-civil-suit-over-alleged-terror-financing/

data.title: Morgan Stanley Seeks National Trust Charter to Custody Crypto
data.description:
Morgan Stanley applies for a national trust charter to custody and manage client crypto, including trading and staking services, marking a major institutional push into regulated digital assets.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/morgan-stanley-seeks-national-trust-charter-to-custody-crypto/

data.title: Short-term profit-taking pushes Bitcoin back below key $70K level – What next?
data.description:
Weak participants and sentiment indicated limited conviction in the rally.
source: https://ambcrypto.com/short-term-profit-taking-pushes-bitcoin-back-below-key-70k-level-what-next/

data.title: European Central Bank Faces Pressure Amid War and Inflation Concerns
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) is under increasing pressure as markets anticipate a potential interest rate hike this year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges the ECB faces in maintaining stability amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and rising inflation. The bank's efforts to project calm are being tested as investors closely monitor economic indicators and policy decisions. The situation underscores the delicate balance the ECB must strike to manage economic growth while addressing inflationary pressures.

data.title: Dubai Regulator VARA Issues Cease and Desist Orders to 2 Crypto Exchanges
data.description:
The local regulator said the two exchanges have been offering trading services without the necessary approval.
source: https://cryptopotato.com/dubai-regulator-vara-issues-cease-and-desist-orders-to-2-crypto-exchanges/

data.title: Prediction Markets Surpassed $60 Billion Volume in 2025
data.description: Stacy Muur posted on X that prediction markets are anticipated to exceed a volume of $60 billion by 2025. However, she noted that most of these markets are primarily designed for information aggregation rather than for trading alpha. This suggests a focus on gathering and analyzing data to forecast outcomes rather than directly facilitating trading activities. The growth in prediction markets reflects an increasing interest in utilizing collective intelligence for decision-making processes.

data.title: India's Rise Determined by Its Own Strength, Says Foreign Minister
data.description: India's Foreign Minister, S. Jaishankar, emphasized that India's ascent on the global stage will be determined by its own capabilities rather than the shortcomings of others. According to Jin10, Jaishankar highlighted that India's growth trajectory will be shaped by its inherent strengths and strategic decisions. This statement underscores India's focus on self-reliance and proactive engagement in international affairs. The minister's remarks reflect a broader sentiment of confidence in India's potential to influence global dynamics through its own efforts and initiatives.

data.title: Tokenized Euros Just Crossed $1 Billion in Market Cap
data.description:
The combined market capitalization of tokenized euro stablecoins across all blockchain networks has surpassed $1 billion for the first time, according to Token Terminal data. The milestone reflects accelerating institutional and retail demand for euro-denominated digital assets outside the traditional banking system.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/tokenized-euros-just-crossed-1-billion-in-market-cap/

data.title: Arab League to Convene Meeting on Iran Attack
data.description: The Arab League is set to hold a meeting on Sunday to discuss the recent attack by Iran, according to Jin10. This information was reported by Agence France-Presse, citing the Assistant Secretary-General of the Arab League. The meeting aims to address the implications of the attack and explore potential responses from member states. The Arab League's decision to convene highlights the regional concern over Iran's actions and their impact on stability in the Middle East.

data.title: U.S. Army Cancels 82nd Airborne Division Exercise Amid Iran Deployment Speculation
data.description: The U.S. Army has decided to cancel a major training exercise for the 82nd Airborne Division headquarters unit, fueling speculation about a possible deployment to Iran. According to NS3.AI, no official orders for deployment have been issued at this time. The U.S. Department of Defense has refrained from commenting on the circulating rumors regarding these plans.

data.title: Bitcoin Whales Sell Off as Retail Investors Buy Amid Price Fluctuations
data.description: Bitcoin whales have sold approximately 66% of the coins they accumulated between February 23 and March 3, following a price increase above $70,000, according to NS3.AI. Retail investors holding less than 0.01 BTC are purchasing as the price dips below $70,000. Santiment suggests this pattern indicates the correction may not be over. The recent downturn also resulted in $348.9 million in net outflows from U.S.-based spot Bitcoin ETFs and caused the Crypto Fear & Greed Index to drop by 6 points to 12, categorized as "Extreme Fear."

data.title: Political Shifts in Britain's Post-Industrial Cities Signal Change
data.description: Britain's post-industrial cities are experiencing a political shift, with voters moving away from centrist positions towards more extreme options on both the left and right. Bloomberg posted on X that this trend was highlighted by the government's third-place finish in last week's Manchester by-election, suggesting potential challenges ahead for the current administration. The results indicate a growing dissatisfaction among voters in these regions, which could have significant implications for future elections and policy directions. As political landscapes evolve, the government may need to reassess its strategies to address the concerns of these communities.

data.title: Emirates Suspends Flights Amid Continued Disruptions
data.description: Emirates, the world's largest airline, has once again suspended flights as of Saturday due to ongoing disruptions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of these interruptions on the airline's operations. The decision to halt flights comes after a series of interceptions that have affected the airline's ability to maintain its schedule. Emirates is working to address the situation and resume normal operations as soon as possible. Passengers are advised to check for updates regarding their flights.

data.title: Iranian President Calls for National Unity Amid Regional Tensions
data.description: On March 7, Iranian President Pezehiziyan delivered a speech urging the nation to unite in defense of Iran, emphasizing that the country will not surrender unconditionally. According to Jin10, Pezehiziyan extended apologies to neighboring countries and announced that the Interim Leadership Committee had approved a decision on March 6 to cease attacks on neighboring nations and refrain from missile launches unless provoked by these countries.

data.title: Iranian President: Unconditional surrender is absolutely impossible; apologizes to neighboring countries.
data.description: Iranian President Pezechian addressed the nation, calling for national unity in defending Iran and stating that Iran would never surrender unconditionally. In his speech, Pezechian apologized to neighboring countries and announced that the interim leadership council had approved on the 6th a cessation of attacks on neighboring countries and missile launches unless those countries launch an attack on Iran first. (CCTV News)

data.title: Chancellor Merz's Conservatives Face Challenge in Regional Election
data.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz's conservative party is encountering an unforeseen obstacle in their efforts to reclaim control from the Greens in a prosperous southwestern region of Germany. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this as the initial test in a series of state elections for the German leader this year. The outcome of this election could have significant implications for the political landscape in Germany, as it sets the tone for upcoming electoral contests. The conservatives are striving to regain influence in a region that has been under Green leadership, reflecting broader national political dynamics.

data.title: Circle Implements Real-Time Settlement with USDC
data.description: Circle, a stablecoin issuer, has initiated internal fund settlements through its own platform, according to PANews. Jeremy Allaire, Circle's CEO, announced on X that the company recently completed inter-company settlements totaling approximately $68 million using USDC and Circle Mint across its eight entities. The entire process took less than 30 minutes and operates 24/7, replacing traditional fiat wire transfers that typically require 1–3 days. This method maintains full control and audit capabilities. Allaire noted that as Circle Mint continues to update, this stablecoin-based real-time settlement model may become available to more enterprises. Currently, about 90% of the company's transfer pricing settlements can be completed within a day.

data.title: Iranian President Asserts No Hostility Towards Gulf Nations
data.description: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi has stated that Iran harbors no hostility towards countries in the Gulf region. According to Jin10, Raisi emphasized the importance of regional cooperation and peaceful coexistence during a recent address. He highlighted Iran's commitment to fostering diplomatic relations and resolving conflicts through dialogue. Raisi's remarks come amid ongoing tensions in the region, where geopolitical dynamics continue to evolve. The Iranian leader reiterated his country's stance on promoting stability and security in the Gulf, urging neighboring nations to engage in constructive discussions. This statement aligns with Iran's broader foreign policy objectives of enhancing regional ties and reducing external influences. Observers note that Raisi's comments may signal a potential shift towards more collaborative regional interactions.
